reasoning:
The recently observed NFCI Leverage Subindex (up to early June 2025) has demonstrated strong and sustained downward momentum, falling steadily from -0.419 in April to -0.512 by June 6. For the shortest horizon (1 week out, Jun 15), this downward momentum heavily influences the probability, making an increase less likely than a decrease or staying flat. FRED's linear trend analysis gives it a ~0.41 probability of increasing by then. 

However, over longer time horizons, this momentum fades and mean reversion becomes the dominant driver. The subindex is constructed to have a mean of 0 over the long term. Since the starting value (-0.512) is considerably below the historical average, we expect that over extended periods (3 to 6 months), the mathematical expectation will drift upward towards zero. Consequently, for the 2025-09-06 and 2025-12-05 deadlines, the prediction surpasses 0.50, reflecting the rising likelihood that the subindex will recover off its local lows due to structural mean-reversion in the index.
